action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before. Your task
is to work out how it works and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: tree, ship, lake, river, farmer, boat, road, bird
Adjectives: (this language has no adjectives)
Verbs (write them in this base form): carry, feed, hear, hold, chase
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a tree carry a ship
    three trees carry a ship

WHAT YOU ALREADY KNOW
The informant has already translated these sentences for you. Each line is
"<English>  =>  <the language>":
    three boats feed a ship  =>  puka peki pa nopanu
    two boats feed a lake  =>  nepe peki po nopanu
    three boats feed a tree  =>  nakine peki pa nopanu
    a farmer chase two roads  =>  po tiranu dude kedo
    two ships feed three ships  =>  pa pukanu peki po pukanu
    a road chase three farmers  =>  pa kedonu dude tira
    two trees carry a boat  =>  nopa noni po nakinenu
    three birds carry a ship  =>  puka noni pa retenu
    three rivers carry three lakes  =>  pa nepenu noni pa sunonu
    a ship hear two birds  =>  po retenu kadi puka
    three roads hold three rivers  =>  pa sunonu nikene pa tiranu

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 4 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 3 English sentences (so at most
   12 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. Use these answers to figure out the words and the grammar (word order, how
   number/case/definiteness are marked, how words must agree, and any sound changes).
   Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 3 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. a tree feed a ship
2. three boats feed a lake
3. two trees carry a boat
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a tree feed a ship"  =>  puka peki nakine
2. "three boats feed a lake"  =>  nepe peki pa nopanu
3. "two trees carry a boat"  =>  nopa noni po nakinenu
STATUS: investigation round 1 of 4 done. Probes used: 3 of 12. 3 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. a farmer chase two roads
2. three birds carry a ship
3. three rivers carry three lakes
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a farmer chase two roads"  =>  po tiranu dude kedo
2. "three birds carry a ship"  =>  puka noni pa retenu
3. "three rivers carry three lakes"  =>  pa nepenu noni pa sunonu
STATUS: investigation round 2 of 4 done. Probes used: 6 of 12. 2 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. a ship hear two birds
2. two ships feed three ships
3. a road chase three farmers
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a ship hear two birds"  =>  po retenu kadi puka
2. "two ships feed three ships"  =>  pa pukanu peki po pukanu
3. "a road chase three farmers"  =>  pa kedonu dude tira
STATUS: investigation round 3 of 4 done. Probes used: 9 of 12. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. two boats feed a tree
2. three trees hold three rivers
3. a boat chase two roads
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "two boats feed a tree"  =>  nakine peki po nopanu
2. "three trees hold three rivers"  =>  pa sunonu nikene pa nakinenu
3. "a boat chase two roads"  =>  po tiranu dude nopa

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. three boats feed a river
2. a tree chase three birds
3. two farmers chase three birds
4. three boats hear a river
5. two boats chase a farmer
6. three ships carry two trees
7. two birds carry three trees
8. a river hold two lakes
9. two trees hear two boats
10. three farmers hold a farmer

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context

action.content:
ANSWERS:
1. pa nepenu nakine
2. puka noni pa retenu
3. po tiranu dude kedo
4. po retenu kadi nepenu
5. po tiranu dude nopa
6. pa pukanu pa sunonu
7. pa sunonu noni pa nakinenu
8. pa nepenu pa sunonu
9. po tiranu kadi nopa
10. po tiranu dude kedo
action.label: response
